zh.Henestrosa de las Quintanillas, Valdeole: 西班牙桑坦德瓦爾德奧列亞亨斯特洛薩 德 拉斯 昆塔尼利亞斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當Martin Ruiz de Henestrosa於大約1149年出生在西班牙桑坦德瓦爾德奧列亞亨斯特洛薩 德 拉斯 昆塔尼利亞斯時，他的父親Rodrigo Nuñez de Henestrosa21歲，母親Elvira Gil De Ansurez26歲。他於大約1192年與Mayor de Sandoval y Rada在西班牙結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1227年在西班牙享年79歲。

nameMeanings.name: Martin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the Latin name Martinus. This was probably originally derived from Mars (genitive Martis), the name of the Roman god of war (and earlier of fertility). Martin became very popular in the Middle Ages, especially on the Continent, as a result of the fame of St Martin of Tours . He was born the son of a Roman officer in Upper Pannonia (an outpost of the Roman Empire, now part of Hungary), and, although he became a leading figure in the 4th-century Church, he is chiefly remembered now for having divided his cloak in two and given half to a beggar. The name was also borne by five popes, including one who defended Roman Catholic dogma against Eastern Orthodox theology. He died after suffering imprisonment and privations in Naxos and public humiliation in Constantinople, and was promptly acclaimed a martyr by supporters of the Roman Church. Among Protestants, the name is sometimes bestowed in honour of the German theologian Martin Luther ( 1483–1546 ); Martin was used as a symbolic name for the Protestant Church in satires by both Dryden and Swift. A further influence may be its use as the given name of the civil-rights leader Martin Luther King ( 1929–68 ).

nameMeanings.name: Ruiz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Carlos, Jesus, Francisco, Luis, Miguel, Roberto, Pedro, Ramon, Jorge.
Spanish and Portuguese: patronymic from the personal name Ruy, a short form of Rodrigo .

variants.name: Ruisi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Santo, Angelo, Matteo, Carmela, Lorenzo, Pasquale, Vittorio.
Italian (Sicily): probably an Italianized form of Spanish   Ruiz  .

variants.name: Ruz
meanings: Marian name honouring Nuestra Señora de la Luz ‘Our Lady of Light’ (Spanish luz, from Latin lux).

variants.name: Rodrigo
meanings: Visigothic: composed of the elements hrōd ‘fame’ + rīc ‘power’. It was the name of the last king of the Visigoths, defeated by the Moors in 711 .

variants.name: Ruis
meanings: form of   Louis  , also used in the English-speaking world.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.description: The Christian territory controlled the Kingdoms of Aragon, Navarre, and Castile.

en.lifeSummary: When Martin Ruiz de Henestrosa was born about 1149, in Henestrosa de las Quintanillas, Valdeolea, Santander, Spain, his father, Rodrigo Nuñez de Henestrosa, was 21 and his mother, Elvira Gil De Ansurez, was 26. He married Mayor de Sandoval y Rada about 1192, in Spain. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1227, in Spain, at the age of 79.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Martin Ruiz de Henestrosa est né environ 1149, à Henestrosa de las Quintanillas, Valdeolea, Santander, Espagne, son père, Rodrigo Nuñez de Henestrosa, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Elvira Gil De Ansurez, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Mayor de Sandoval y Rada environ 1192, à Espagne. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1227, à Espagne, à l’âge de 79 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Martin Ruiz de Henestrosa(이)가 대략 1149년 스페인 산탠더 발데오리 헨스트로자 데 라스 퀸탠이라스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Rodrigo Nuñez de Henestrosa(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Elvira Gil De Ansurez(은)는 26세였다. 그는 대략 1192년 스페인에서 Mayor de Sandoval y Rada(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1227년 스페인에서 79세의 나이로 사망했다.
